name: Jolly Jumbuck in a Tuckerbag
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon dried mint leaves or 2 leaves fresh mint
1/2 teaspoon dried rosemary or 1 sprig fresh
1/2 teaspoon dried parsley or 1 sprig fresh
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim fat from lamb and cut meat into cubes.
2. Chop meat with onion and herbs in a food processor.
3. Add egg, bread crumbs, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper. Mix together.
4. Form the mixture into 4 patties.
5. Heat oil in a frying pan. Sear patties on both sides until browned but not cooked through. Cool patties on paper towels and refrigerate until cool to the touch.
6. Roll thawed pastry dough to 1/8 inch thick.
7. Cut pastry into 4 circles, approximately 8 inches in diameter.
8. Place a cleaned rack bone (from the lamb) into the center of each patty.
9. Place patties on puff pastry circles and draw up sides of pastry around bones.
10. Cut a small space at the top of "bags" for steam to vent.
11. Tie the top of each "bag" with a piece of cooked spaghetti.
12. Place pastry bags on lightly greased cake racks over a baking sheet.
13. Make an egg wash using 1 egg yolk and 1 tablespoon milk.
14. Brush egg wash on the bags.
15. Bake in a 450°F oven for 10 to 15 minutes. Serve immediately on warmed plates with mixed vegetables.
Serves 4.
